tasks.title: REA-DRT-07 — Bridge SSR cookie to browser supabase-js session (Realtime never initializes)
tasks.description: Root cause (investigated 2026-05-19): server-side cookie defaults force httpOnly: true on every cookie they write — including the sb-<ref>-auth-token chunks. src/utils/supabase/server.ts:10-16 and src/utils/supabase/middleware.ts:6-12 both define { httpOnly: true, ...options } defaults, and @supabase/ssr doesn't override httpOnly, so the auth cookies land HttpOnly. The browser supabase-js client (src/utils/supabase/client.ts:19-28) reads cookies via document.cookie, which by spec cannot see HttpOnly cookies — so getSession() returns null, Realtime channels never authenticate, and TokenRefreshService throws REFRESH_FAILED (the symptom #404 patched as graceful failure). Regression introduced in commit f49f6ec5 (2026-01-07 mobile-Safari ITP fix); the sameSite: 'lax' + request+response sync changes in that commit were correct, only httpOnly: true was wrong. Fix: drop httpOnly: true from getDefaultCookieOptions (server.ts) and getCookieOptions (middleware.ts); auth cookies must be JS-readable for the @supabase/ssr SSR↔browser bridge. ~2-line change in 2 files. Add a browser getSession() smoke test in src/lib/auth/__tests__/token-refresh-and-session.test.ts that fails if auth cookies are written HttpOnly. Tradeoff: XSS-exfiltrable auth tokens in principle; mitigated by existing CSP + dompurify + Supabase's short-lived JWT + refresh-token rotation. Keep #404 patch in place — graceful sign-in redirect on legitimate session expiry is still correct behavior. Out of scope: Realtime reconnect handler (see docs/architecture/REMEDIATION_PLAN.md). See docs/JOURNAL.md 2026-05-19 for full trace.

tasks.title: REA-367 — Realtime channel.off() cleanup throws (channel closes ~1ms after subscribe)
tasks.description: RealtimeChannelManager.off() (src/lib/realtime/channels.ts:168-173) calls this.channel.off('broadcast', { event }, listener), but the Supabase RealtimeChannel has no runtime .off() — that's a Phoenix Channels method present only via a .d.ts type augmentation. So cleanup throws TypeError: this.channel.off is not a function; the catch only swallows 'closed' / 'not found' / 'already removed' messages, so it logs a WARN and the channel tears down — the admin driver-locations channel reaches subscribed then closes ~1ms later. Fix: drop the per-event off() path and tear down via client.removeChannel(channel) / channel.unsubscribe() (supabase-js v2 has no per-callback removal). Confirmed live during REA-DRT-07 verification on 2026-05-20 (channel subscribed → cleanup TypeError → closed). Blocks DRT-06+. Promoted from QA fail REA-DRT-05 (sub-step DT5-01, Critical).
